content.rendered:
Few artists have had to stage a comeback like Lucinda Williams.

In January 2020, a tornado ripped through her home. We all know what happened in the weeks and months that followed. And if losing a home and a global pandemic wasn’t enough, in November of that year she suffered a stroke that paralyzed the left side of her body.

She had to learn to walk all over again, but  when it was time to record her next album, she still couldn’t play her guitar. That made songwriting a challenge. “I’ve had to have another guitar player try to play the way I would play,” she told Classic Rock in a 2023 interview. “I think of melodies in my head a lot, but mostly it’s been lyrics. ”

Fortunately, Lucinda has always been able to write great lyrics.

Although the album ended up titled Stories from a Rock N Roll Heart, its thematic heart is arguably the album’s second track, “New York Comeback,” which features backing vocals by her friends Bruce Springsteen and Patti Scialfa.

Bruce was recruited for the album by Jesse Malin, a mutual friend who co-wrote the track with Lucinda. Malin, Williams, and her husband Tom Overby were sitting around the table one day when Overby wistfully remarked, “Wouldn’t it be great to get Bruce on these songs?”

“You must realize that Jesse is the unofficial mayor of the music scene in New York City,” Lucinda remarked in the same interview. “He knows everybody. Jesse said, ‘I’ll get hold of Bruce,’ and he did it. It thrills me every time I hear Bruce’s voice on the record. I still can’t get over it.”

Tragedy and irony coincided barely a month after the release of “New York Comeback” when Malin suffered a stroke of his own, completely paralyzing him from the waist down. Lucinda stood by her friend, appearing on a benefit tribute album and at live benefit shows on his behalf.

Like Williams, Malin has staged his own New York comeback. Both artists are now walking and performing again, and as for their song: “New York Comeback” made it all the way to Number One on the American charts.

New York Comeback
Recorded: 2022
Released: Stories From a Rock N Roll Heart (2023)
Never performed

© July 8, 2026

jetpack-related-posts.title: Disgusted
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce joined Lucinda Williams and her band on stage in London to perform a cover of Melvin Jackson's "Disgusted."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Lucinda Williams: Factory
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Lucinda Williams covers Bruce in concert often, but she's only covered him in the studio once. Take a listen to her version of "Factory" from her 2016 album, The Ghost of Highway 20.

jetpack-related-posts.title: November 10
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Not one but *two* Springsteen box sets were released on this date: Live 1975-85 and Tracks. Plus: a Billy Joel guest appearance, a Daily Show interview, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, The Deep Dark Woods: Factory
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Canadian band Deep Dark Woods turns in a faithful cover of Bruce's minimalist musing on the meaning of work.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, The Mandrillos: Cross My Heart
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: "Cross My Heart" always had more potential than its album arrangement allowed for, and this three-man combo proves it.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice: I Gotta Be Free
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Short on lyrics, long on guitar jams, "I Gotta Be Free" is an early Springsteen original from his Steel Mill period with a mid-song cover of The Grateful Dead's "Turn on Your Love Light."
